items.title: Stand up local MAE frontend

sections.title: 🎯 Today's single side-project focus (D009)
sections.content: **morning-brief** is today's side project. Crumbs, BedRock, and Gestgo are **frozen** until tomorrow — no PRs, no commits, no context switches. MAE (day job) runs in parallel, independent of this rule.

items.title: Jerry's OKR (manager)
items.desc: 2026 Q2 O1: 95%+ stability, 80%+ expected-rate. O3 (Jun's line): automated eval for sub-agent / single-prompt AB diff. Scoring 0.6–0.7 = on-target.

items.title: Jun's Eval-Driven Development (mentor)
items.desc: 4-layer eval definition + AI-executed loop: analyze → locate → fix → verify → deploy → regression. Human only decides. L3 LLM-Judge 8-dim framework exists, pending CI integration.

items.desc: Eval × Ralph Loop × Skills × PPE = four-piece kit. Workbench: git worktree + superpowers + gstack. Typical day: local iterate → /ppe push → regression → autofix.

items.title: Eval Frontend (live)
items.desc: Your eval history dashboard — branch mae/develop deploys here.

item.title: Agent-as-a-Judge: Evaluate Agents with Agents
item.authors: Zhuge, Zhao, Ashley, Wang, Khizbullin, Xiong, Liu, Chang, Krishnamoorthi, Tian, Shi, Chandra, Schmidhuber
item.tldr: Outcome-only eval misses the step-by-step nature of agentic systems; manual trace eval doesn't scale. Framework wraps another agent as a judge that inspects intermediate steps, not just final output.
item.why: Directly relevant to MAE's LLM-Judge layer — MAE today has 10 LLM judges scoring outputs but diagnostics module (v2.2, April 16) is moving toward step-level evidence (Text / Effect Agent 3-way). Agent-as-Judge formalizes exactly that direction.
key_findings: Step-level judgment catches failures that final-output scoring hides
Agent-as-Judge matches or exceeds human eval on agent benchmarks at ~a fraction of cost
Useful for DevAI / SWE-Bench-style agent pipelines — same shape as MAE's video agent pipeline

items.title: Qwen3.6-27B: Flagship-Level Coding in a 27B Dense Model
items.summary: Alibaba claims coding parity with much larger models at 27B dense.
items.why: Small-model coding is relevant if MAE ever needs cheaper sub-agents for skill selection / description_similarity — the Rule evaluators don't need a frontier model.

items.title: Parallel agents in Zed
items.summary: Zed editor now runs multiple coding agents in parallel on the same repo with conflict mediation.
items.why: Close cousin of Ralph Loop + worktree pattern in MAE dev paradigm. Worth seeing how Zed handles merge contention — MAE uses git worktree today.

items.title: Anthropic postmortem: Claude Code quality regression
items.summary: Root-cause writeup on the Claude Code quality dip users noticed this week.
items.why: You use Claude Code inside the MAE /autofix loop. Knowing the failure mode protects your eval regressions from being falsely attributed to your own prompt changes.
